# AnyVali

> Native validation libraries for 10 languages, one portable schema model.

AnyVali is a family of native validation libraries that share a single portable schema model across 10 programming languages: JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Go, Java, C#, Rust, PHP, Ruby, Kotlin, and C++.

Write schemas in your host language using idiomatic builder APIs, export them to a canonical JSON document, and import them in any other supported SDK. All SDKs pass a shared conformance test suite ensuring identical behavior.

- Website: https://anyvali.com
- Documentation: https://docs.anyvali.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/BetterCorp/AnyVali

## Documentation

For full API reference, specification, and guides, see the documentation site:

- [Full LLM-optimised docs](https://docs.anyvali.com/llms.txt)
- [Overview](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/overview/)
- [Normative Spec](https://docs.anyvali.com/spec/spec/)
- [JSON Interchange Format](https://docs.anyvali.com/spec/json-format/)
- [Portability Guide](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/portability-guide/)
- [Numeric Semantics](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/numeric-semantics/)
- [SDK Authors Guide](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/sdk-authors-guide/)
- [CLI Reference](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/cli/)
- [HTTP API Reference](https://docs.anyvali.com/docs/api/)

## Install

```
npm install @anyvali/js          # JavaScript / TypeScript
pip install anyvali              # Python
go get github.com/BetterCorp/AnyVali/sdk/go  # Go
cargo add anyvali                # Rust
dotnet add package AnyVali       # C#
composer require anyvali/anyvali # PHP
gem install anyvali              # Ruby
```

## Quick Example (TypeScript)

```typescript
import { object, string, int64, importSchema } from "@anyvali/js";

const User = object({
  id: int64(),
  email: string().format("email"),
});

const result = User.safeParse({ id: 42, email: "team@anyvali.dev" });
const doc = User.export(); // portable JSON
const imported = importSchema(doc); // import in any SDK
```
